other_observers: Mike Henwood; 11/09/2006 by Forrest Luke. He noted: "I barely had any light left so I just pulled up on the sidewalk and got out my scope. I found the bird after a couple of minutes. It was a little more difficult because it was sleeping with its head t
specific_location: Pond ~1/4 mile north of Colorado River on east side of 29 Road
description: Head red with golden "pate" and forehead. Faint hint of geenish mask. Bill blue with black tip. Back and sides gay; horizontal white slash on side. Undertail coverts black; white patch on flank separating black of coverts from gray sides.Behaviors: Swimming liesurely and resting. Nothing different from American Wigeons.Call: nonePlumage: alternate gray morph
